entry.title: JoongAng Daily: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

  • SBS commissions second season of hit series 'Taxi Driver'

  • The legacy of a father (KOR)
The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.title: NY Times: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • As Survivors of Gun Violence Demand Action, House Passes Bill Doomed in the Senate
  • ‘We Ask for Progress’: The Parents of an Uvalde Victim Demand Stricter Gun Laws
  • From the Graveside to the Front, Ukrainians Tell of Grim Endurance

  • The Pandemic Crushed Teachers. After Uvalde, They Wonder ‘What More?’

  • Military Plane Carrying 5 Marines Crashes in California, Officials Say
  • Brookings Puts Retired Gen. John Allen on Leave Amid Lobbying Inquiry
  • Jan. 6 Inquiry Votes Aren’t Costing G.O.P. Incumbents in Primaries, Yet
  • Oris Buckner, Detective Who Blew Whistle on Police Abuse, Dies at 70

entry.title: Guardian: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • NBA finals Game 3: Golden State Warriors 100-116 Boston Celtics – live!
  • Global supply chain crisis fuels push to local manufacturing as China’s appeal dims
  • Russia-Ukraine war: what we know on day 106 of the invasion
  • Uvalde survivor, 11, tells House hearing she smeared herself with friend’s blood
  • Beneath what’s become the hidden pandemic lie tales of pain and anguish | Paul Daley
  • Sex, laughs and the snip: an audience with Thailand’s ‘Condom King’
  • Twitter set to comply with Elon Musk demand for data on fake accounts
  • ‘It’s a war on the people’: El Salvador’s mass arrests send thousands into despair

  • Trials of new Covid vaccine raise hopes of once-a-year booster
  • Pelé joins calls for Brazil to step up search for pair missing in Amazon
  • Harvey Weinstein to be charged with indecent assault in UK
  • ‘She is dancing among the greats’: the dangerously honest, richly ambiguous Paula Rego
  • Tory rebels wait for Boris Johnson to ‘blow himself up’ to trigger fresh vote
  • MEPs reject key EU climate proposal after aims watered down
  • Six ways with Asian greens: ‘They’re almost like a cross between spinach and broccoli’
  • Canada: two died as police sought approval to tweet 2020 mass shooting warning
  • Ukraine forces pushed back to outskirts of Sievierodonetsk, say Donbas leaders
  • 100-year-old Italian woman has driving licence renewed
  • The Phil Mickelson Circus rolls into golf’s breakaway LIV Series | Ewan Murray
  • Jack Leach ready for England action but Ollie Robinson out of Test series
  • Liverpool ready to pay £85m for Núñez but reject Bayern’s new Mané bid
  • Alok Sharma in running to be UN’s global climate chief
  • Al-Qaida in Indian subcontinent plans revenge attacks over prophet remarks
  • West to rebuke Iran over lack of cooperation with nuclear watchdog
  • Fatal police shooting of woman in Paris sparks political row
  • Trevor Noah on New York passing stricter gun laws: ‘I didn’t even know that was possible’
  • Goya’s horrific Black Paintings are brought to life – La Quinta del Sordo review
  • Decision on lifting EU sanctions against Alisher Usmanov and sisters expected soon
  • Artist Paula Rego, known for her visceral and unsettling work, dies aged 87
  • My 98-year-old friend fled Nuremberg on the Kindertransport. Today would he be sent to Rwanda? | Chitra Ramaswamy
  • ‘It’s like oxygen – it’s everywhere!’ Why Korea is hot for trot, the cheesiest pop imaginable
  • My mother in the garden swamped by green – Lydia Goldblatt’s best photograph
  • In need of light relief? Top Gun: Maverick is a reminder that Tom Cruise has still got it | Emma Brockes
  • Bruno Pereira: the dedicated defender of Indigenous rights missing in Brazil
  • Yulia Tymoshenko on war in Ukraine: ‘It’s a chance for the free world to kill this evil’
  • I’m a Black gun owner. I have mixed feelings about gun control | Akin Olla
  • Ms Marvel review – a glorious debut for the MCU’s first Muslim superhero
  • Warned of ‘massive’ climate-led extinction, a US energy firm funded crisis denial ads
  • How to make coronation chicken – recipe | Felicity Cloake's masterclass
  • ‘I never lost the joy!’: singer Gilbert O’Sullivan on love, loss and lawsuits
  • The best games of 2022 so far
  • ‘Gold rush’ for gas production threatens to lock in global heating
  • The split over Boris Johnson’s future is just the start of a massive Tory identity crisis | Rafael Behr
  • EasyJet diverted my flight and then refused to pay my taxi bill
  • A moment that changed me: I lost my home, moved into a van – and found freedom
  • I’m nearly 60. Here’s what I’ve learned about growing old | Tim Dowling
  • Government failure to boost energy efficiency ‘inexplicable’, says IEA
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 9 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-09 03:31AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • 'I've delivered a report card I do not regret', Hong Kong's Carrie Lam says in emotional speech in last Legco session
  • Hong Kong set to double levy on plastic bags, charge producers and suppliers extra for glass beverage bottles in drive to reduce waste
  • Coronavirus: Aji Ichiban co-founder may lower rents for vacant shops after Covid-19 losses forced once-thriving Hong Kong snack chain to shut down
  • Look beyond dollar signs to see Jumbo Floating Restaurant’s true value to Hong Kong
  • Hong Kong’s Fire Services Department readies 2,000 additional officers to handle possible 6th Covid-19 wave, in bid to ease strain on paramedics
  • Hong Kong universities’ international reputation takes a hit in latest world rankings, but result still ‘broadly positive’
  • Ex-worker, parents charged in HK$900,000 bribes-for-kindergarten places case at Hong Kong ESF school
  • From cage homes to subdivided flats — what life is like in Hong Kong’s worst living spaces
  • Hong Kong police break up another money-laundering syndicate, arresting 16 linked to HK$2.5 billion in suspected crime proceeds
  • Hong Kong’s John Lee seeks legal exemptions for failing to comply with election rules during leadership bid
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong logs 558 new infections, even after officials tighten rules on caseloads
  • What is the MPF offsetting mechanism and why are workers happy the Hong Kong government is set to finally scrap it?
  • Fudan medical degree first from mainland China to be recognised in Hong Kong in special scheme, among 23 more overseas schools listed
  • Top advisers to Hong Kong leader defend decision to leave discussions on controversial hefty pay rise for civil servants to next administration
  • Woman arrested for outraging public decency after video of couple having sex on Hong Kong flat balcony posted online
  • Rescuers search for missing woman who fell into stream while hiking, as Hong Kong suspends afternoon classes amid rainstorm warning
  • Choosing French as a way to open doors
  • Video: Large sinkhole discovered in southern China, the 30th of its kind in the same county
  • China trade: export growth rebounds, imports also beat expectations
  • Video: Chinese scientists develop robot worm to explore engine pipes
  • Chinese blockchain platform ChainMaker says it has new technology to keep it secure from quantum attacks
  • Man in China seeks divorce from wife of 16 years after paternity tests reveal 3 daughters he raised are not his children
  • ‘It’s tough’: lockdown drags on for millions in Dandong, near North Korea
  • Three US companies sanctioned for sending defence blueprints to China
  • With Xinjiang top of mind, EU lawmakers urge more hardline approach on China human rights
  • China’s positions crucial in crunch talks as WTO chief eyes deals on fishing and vaccines
  • US confrontation or Chinese cooperation? As the Asia-Pacific contemplates its future security, the choice is obvious
  • US mute on China in unveiling ‘ambitious’ Latin America plans at Summit of the Americas
  • China defends its veto of UN Security Council resolution sanctioning North Korea
  • Janet Yellen says Joe Biden’s team looking to ‘reconfigure’ China tariffs
  • Will China’s rail link between Xinjiang, Kyrgyzstan and Uzbekistan change the great power rivalry in Central Asia?
  • China’s FAST telescope detects mystery radio flashes from 3 billion light years away, with energy to rival the sun
  • Taiwan reports record Covid-19 deaths, spike in local cases
  • China’s Central Asia pledges made with eye on both US and Moscow, analysts say
  • China’s answer to open-source software giant Red Hat says its boss was taken away for disciplinary investigation
  • China’s transport restrictions have upended goods trade, Premier Li says in calling for logistical changes
  • What next for China’s zero-Covid policy after Shanghai lockdown?
  • Nike’s running app to stop service in China next month, following similar moves by Airbnb and Kindle
  • Taiwan expands intelligence operations centre to keep tabs on PLA
  • Shanghai reopening: Tesla’s Gigafactory 3 to exit ‘closed loop’ on June 11 as Covid-19 fears ease
  • China hails ‘ironclad partnership’ with Cambodia as work begins on naval base, raising US concern
  • ByteDance’s private valuation falls by US$100 billion in one year as IPO plans remain up in the air
  • Bon Odori Festival: Malaysian royal intervention saves Japan dance festival from Muslim critics
  • Indonesian navy officers asked for US$375,000 to release tanker off its waters near Singapore
  • Japan’s June 10 reopening: tourism sector braces for surge of travel-starved visitors eager to ‘experience, not see’ the country
  • Malaysia 1MDB scandal: Ex-PM Najib Razak’s wife Rosmah Mansor gives up claim on diamond bracelet seized in 2018 raid
  • Malaysia’s tourism sector stretched thin amid deluge of post-Covid travellers from Singapore, Indonesia, India and Europe
  • Japanese man arrested in Indonesia over Covid relief fraud, estimated at US$7.3 million
  • Thai dissident attacker jailed in Japan for breaking into home and using tear gas
  • Philippine banana growers plead for Japanese consumers to bear price hikes – farmers on brink of bankruptcy
  • Thailand’s ‘Ferrari’ police officer gets life for brutal torture and death of suspect
  • Vietnam’s health minister arrested over price gouging of Covid-19 tests
  • Thai railway market beloved by durian-hunting Chinese tourists makes a post-Covid comeback – even if they haven’t
  • Bon Odori Festival: Malaysian minister tells Muslims to avoid ‘un-Islamic’ Japanese dance event
  • New Zealand plans to charge farmers for sheep and cow burps to curb methane emissions
  • Thailand decriminalises cannabis on Thursday. What’s going to change?
  • No peace for South Korea’s Moon Jae-in, or fellow retirees, as noisy, rude protesters bombard his now-not-so-sleepy village
  • WHO warns of ‘real’ risk as monkeypox outbreak tops 1,000 cases
  • Calls grow to boycott Indian goods in Mideast following insults of Prophet Muhammad and Islam by members of Narendra Modi’s BJP
  • Iran switches off nuclear site cameras as IAEA censure adopted
  • Belgium’s king expresses regret, but offers no apology for colonial past in landmark Congo trip
  • US Olympians and dozens of other women sue FBI over botched Larry Nassar sex abuse probe
  • Girl, 11, tells US Congress how she covered herself in blood to survive Texas school shooting
  • Corruption-hit British Virgin Islands agrees to reforms, avoiding UK rule for now
  • Moderna’s Omicron-specific Covid-19 vaccine shows positive results
  • Five steps global policymakers can take to provide supply solutions to stagflation
  • Teacher killed as man drives car into crowd in Germany, others critically injured
  • Russian army base scrambling for Ukraine war supplies, locals and soldiers say
  • Russia’s Aeroflot plans US$3 billion cash injection, said to eye big jet order
  • No breakthrough after Russia’s Lavrov in Türkiye for talks on Ukraine grain exports
  • As US Capitol riot public hearings begin, who will be held accountable?
  • Brazil police open criminal probe amid search for British journalist Dom Phillips in Amazon jungle
  • Hong Kong stocks drop before China trade data as tech leaders limp while BYD and CNOOC advance on demand outlook
  • Sunac, China’s fourth-largest developer by sales, to delay payment of US$345 million onshore bond due on Monday, sources say
  • Twitter will give Elon Musk ‘firehose’ of raw tweet data in dispute over bots, reports say
  • Alibaba’s cloud services business launches two new data centres in Saudi Arabia to step up its overseas expansion
  • Hedge fund group files complaint against LME over March nickel trading chaos
  • Why it is in US interests to give up its dollar privilege to forge a more neutral financial order
  • Baidu’s Jidu Auto releases virtual concept robocar as intelligent EV unit moves towards mass production in 2023
  • Hong Kong IPO forecast slashed in half, as PwC expects 2022 proceeds to shrink 40 per cent amid tumbling valuations
  • China welcomes ‘important’ capital inflows with economy still reeling from coronavirus outbreaks
  • Huawei pursues more patent-licensing deals for 4G, 5G mobile technologies to boost sales amid struggles with US sanctions
  • A World Data Organisation needed to avoid rules-based disorder
  • Chinese tech stocks are either a good buy or a trap in market slump, investors say
  • British wealth manager SJP eyes China’s growing pension market, details retirement risk for Hong Kong’s ‘sandwiched generation’
  • Overseas traders return to Chinese onshore stocks, tucking into blue chips amid longest buying streak this year
  • Chip-making tools firm ASML to hire 200 staff in China as Covid restrictions ease, including sanctions compliance role
  • Elon Musk’s Twitter deal draws lawsuit from investor with five shares seeking info about bots
  • China R&D key part of ‘global portfolio’, but technology leaks remain concern, report says
  • It takes a hardy tourist to visit Sri Lanka amid protests and power cuts
  • How AI, data are transforming healthcare: three key trends that could change the face of medicine
  • Kristen Stewart and fiancée Dylan Meyer are one of Hollywood’s most stylish couples
  • How reading Stephen Covey’s The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People changed the life of NGO Light Be founder Ricky Yu
  • In K-drama Why Her? Seo Hyun-jin plays a vicious lawyer who falls from grace. Will she recover – and show us some human foibles?
  • Hong Kong art exhibition of Francis Bacon, George Condo and other artists’ portraits asks: what does it mean when one’s face ceases to be recognisable?
  • Unveiling new Dior chair, Philippe Starck predicts end of design: ‘Look at your iPhone – the number of products it replaces, it’s extraordinary’
  • Diabetes drug could be weight-loss miracle: patients shed up to 21 per cent of their body weight in trial of tirzepatide
  • Cherry Magic! movie review: Japanese boys’ love drama forgets the humour of its TV original, and is an ordeal to watch
  • How accessible design turned a Hong Kong apartment into a safe, wheelchair-friendly home for elderly couple to ‘comfortably grow a little older’
  • On Her Watch: Why high jewellery and Métiers d’Art timepieces are the ultimate luxury for women, from Chanel’s Mademoiselle Privé collection to Bruguet and Jaquet Droz watches
  • The extravagant lives of India’s disgraced Gupta family: from mansions in Dubai and South Africa, and Lamborghini and Porsche supercars, to ‘big fat Gupta’ weddings, Atul and Rajesh splashed millions
  • Stranger friends: how Millie Bobby Brown and Noah Schnapp became besties IRL, from meeting on the set of Netflix series Stranger Things and red carpet appearances, to making viral TikToks together
  • Meet Austin Butler, the King of Rock in the new Elvis biopic: the former Disney star sings and plays guitar and is dating Kaia Gerber – but it was ex Vanessa Hudgens who said he should play Presley
  • What will Johnny Depp do next? He’s set for a career comeback amid rumours of Beetlejuice 2 and Pirates of the Caribbean film returns, his Dior luxury endorsements and new music with Jeff Beck
  • Who’s the daddy? Celeb kids with secret parents, from Amber Heard’s baby Oonagh Paige and Geri Horner’s firstborn Bluebell, to Mindy Kaling’s two children and Cristiano Ronaldo’s son Jr.
  • Why rare gems are breaking records at auction in Hong Kong: Christie’s and Sotheby’s natural coloured jewels are raking in millions, including a vivid blue diamond that fetched US$57.47 million
  • The 12 best French restaurants in Hong Kong, from three-Michelin-starred Caprice at Four Seasons Hong Kong to the city’s first fine dining restaurant, Gaddi’s
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Washington Post: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: U.N. seeks deal on food exports as war drives up prices and hurts millions worldwide
  • Car crashes into Berlin crowd, killing 1; driver is detained
  • Top general details plan to train Ukrainians on rocket artillery
  • Colombia discovers two shipwrecks, releases new images of sunken treasure
  • Song Hae, a South Korean TV host with staying power, dies at 95
  • The scene after a car crashed into a Berlin crowd, killing 1 and injuring several more
  • Inside the Taliban’s secret war in the Panjshir Valley
  • She was raped in Ukraine. How many others have stories like hers?
  • Merkel defends Ukraine policy, does not regret engaging Russia
  • Fiji court lets U.S. seize Russian oligarch’s $300 million superyacht
  • Severodonetsk defenders holding out under merciless shelling, mayor says
  • Lawyers for migrants say U.S. officials slowed family reunifications
  • Britain’s Johnson may be sinking at home, but he has fans in Ukraine
  • China and Russia defend North Korea vetoes in first at UN
  • Iran says 2 UN watchdog devices at nuclear site turned off
  • Activists try giant screen to clean up Guatemalan river
  • Brazil sends helicopters to search for 2 missing in Amazon
  • EXPLAINER: Crime, impunity surge in Amazon's Javari Valley
  • Documents: Dominican minister slain over denied permits
  • UN report: Ukraine war is increasing suffering of millions
  • Town evacuating, 3 firefighters hurt in wildfire in Spain
  • Venezuelans big presence in caravan after visa requirement
  • Belgian king expresses 'deepest regrets' to Congolese
  • Military aircraft carrying five Marines crashes in California
  • There have been over 250 mass shootings so far in 2022
  • Man arrested after hurling woman onto NYC subway tracks, police say
  • Miah’s shrug and the unbearable weight of gun violence
  • Mom of Buffalo shooting victim testifies, ‘This is exactly who we are’
  • 5 percent of young adults identify as trans or nonbinary, survey says
  • AR-15s useful to shoot prairie dogs, GOP leader says, as gun talks intensify
  • Man sues American Airlines, says mistake led to 17-day jail ‘nightmare’
  • DNA ties ‘Pillowcase Rapist’ to six cold cases, officials say
  • 10-year-old in Fla. fatally shot woman arguing with her mom, police say
  • ‘Chrisley Knows Best’ reality TV stars convicted of fraud, tax evasion
  • Tracking Biden’s nominees to fill the top roles in his administration
  • Post Politics Now: Gun violence victims testify in House; Biden to host Summit of the Americas
  • Charles Kernaghan, who crusaded against sweatshops, dies at 74
  • Empty wallets, empty tanks: Surging gas prices leave drivers stranded
  • The covid economy is harder for Black Americans
  • What is stagflation? Here’s why it matters and what you should know.
  • Help Desk Quick Fix: how to save your precious voice mails
  • Ex-‘World of Warcraft’ developers unveil game in deal with Twitch stars
  • In reversal, Twitter plans to comply with Musk’s demands for data
  • ‘League of Legends’ no longer exclusive to PlayVS for high schools
  • Distinguished homes for sale in the D.C. region
  • Manassas Park embraces its youth, diversity and community spirit
  • Homeowner wishes to remove ex-spouse from mortgage without refinancing
  • Should you consider an adjustable-rate mortgage?
  • In the Oil Market, the Strong Dollar Is the World’s Problem
  • The US Should Be Paying More Attention to the Pacific
  • India’s Central Bank Is Getting Its Way on Rates. But For How Long?
  • It Took Forever to Get to 2%. Now That’s Too High
  • Meet the Guptas, Symbols of South African Corruption
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • All About Mohammed bin Salman, the Prince at the Center of US-Saudi Reset
  • What the Jan. 6 Committee Has Done, and What’s Next
  • Centrists’ new war on crime is also a war on the left
  • When the pronoun police come for eighth-graders
  • It’s time to confront Putin’s destructive war on grain.
  • As climate catastrophes multiply, Republicans rethink denialism
  • Addressing voter anger about crime doesn’t have to mean abandoning reform
  • ‘The Wire’ is 20. ‘We Own This City’ is a challenge to its biggest fans.
  • I ran for office full of anger and despair. I felt hope when I dropped out.
  • Please, Democrats, no showhorses at the Jan. 6 hearings
  • The federal budget has 99 problems. This program ain’t one — anymore.
  • Religion is not overpowering our politics
  • Let’s be honest: On guns, the enemy is us
  • Boudin’s recall proves Democrats have lost the public’s trust on crime
  • Congress must find the courage shown by families affected by gun violence
  • By opposing highway expansions, Maryland politicians risk more than gridlock
  • What happens when judges misbehave? Not enough.
  • Crime needs to be more than just a gun issue for Biden
  • The debate over Democrats and crime is disastrously confused
  • It’s true our Hawaiian Islands are overrun — and not just by chickens
  • Bob Casey: I changed my stance on guns. I shouldn’t be the only one.
  • Cartoon by Dana Summers
  • The unexpected group of people thrilled with Boris Johnson's survival
  • A blunt lesson in a Montgomery graduation speech
  • Montgomery and the state must do something about Old Georgetown Road
  • Permanent changes to gun laws are needed. Now.
  • Avoiding blackouts in an energy crisis
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Financial Times: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
